title: 14 Iranian soldiers killed in unexploded ordnance operations in Zanjan province
summary: Fourteen Iranian soldiers died during operations to defuse unexploded ordnance in northwestern Zanjan province, underscoring the ongoing domestic military toll of the US-Israel conflict.
contents:
## Iranian Military Casualties in Ordnance Disposal

### Incident
Fourteen Iranian soldiers died on May 1 during operations to defuse unexploded ordnance (UXO) in Iran's northwestern Zanjan province, according to local media reports.

### Context
The casualties highlight the persistent threat posed by unexploded munitions from US and Israeli strikes during Operation Epic Fury, which commenced on February 28. Ordnance disposal operations continue across multiple Iranian provinces as the military works to clear hazardous areas.

### Military Impact Assessment
The incident represents a significant single-day loss for Iran's military engineering and EOD (Explosive Ordnance Disposal) capabilities. Such operations carry inherent risk, particularly when dealing with precision-guided munitions that may have failed to detonate on impact.

### Broader Casualty Picture
The UXO casualties add to Iran's growing conflict toll. Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has urged economic resilience against enemies as the combined effects of war and sanctions take their toll on the population and military.

### Regional Significance
The continued UXO threat in Iranian territory demonstrates the lasting physical impact of the aerial campaign, even during the current ceasefire period.
